title: ICU skin pressure area review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

follow_up.text: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.
follow_up.rationale: Supports documented mobility, nutrition, cognition, sensation and skin assessment for ICU skin and pressure-area review; no clinical action is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — adult risk and skin assessment

follow_up.text: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.
follow_up.rationale: Supports clinician-recorded ulcer dimensions, depth, undermining and category for ICU skin and pressure-area review; no clinical action is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 and 1.5.1–1.5.3 — ulcer measurement and categorisation

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Risk assessment, skin site, colour, moisture, temperature, integrity, pain, dimensions, category, devices and trend are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, escalation or disposition is generated for ICU skin and pressure-area review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
